sections.summary: Title and metadata for the Standard for Samsø (CXS 268-1966) with adoption, revision and amendment history (adopted 1966; revised 2007; amended 2008, 2010, 2013, 2018, 2019, 2022).

Records the 2022 amendment replacing the previous labelling provisions for non-retail containers with a requirement to follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
States the scope: this Standard applies to Samsø intended for direct consumption or for further processing as described in Section 2.

Defines Samsø as a ripened hard cheese with near white to light yellow colour, firm texture suitable for cutting, characteristic pea-to-cherry sized gas holes (mostly up to 20 mm), flat cylindrical/square/rectangular shape, sold with or without a hard dry rind or coating; ripening is normally about 3 weeks at 8–17°C to develop flavour and body, though alternative ripening methods may be used if similar properties result, and cheese for further processing need not reach the same degree of ripening.

Section heading introducing essential composition and quality factors and indicating the following subtopics: raw materials, permitted ingredients and composition (with detailed tables in subsections).
Specifies permitted raw materials: cows' milk, buffaloes' milk, their mixtures and products derived from these milks.

Lists permitted ingredients including harmless starter and other cultures, rennet or suitable coagulating enzymes, sodium or potassium chloride, potable water, safe ripening enzymes and processing aids; rice, corn and potato flours/starches may be used as anti-caking agents only for surface treatment of cut, sliced and shredded products in amounts functionally necessary under Good Manufacturing Practice, with a footnote clarifying rindless cheese and ripening film.

Sets compositional requirements: minimum milk fat in dry matter 30% (reference 45–55%); minimum dry matter varies with fat-in-dry-matter — 46% for 30–<40%, 52% for 40–<45%, 54% for 45–<55%, and 59% for ≥55% — and compositional modifications beyond these minima/maxima are not compliant with Section 4.3.3 of the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999).

Permits only those additive functional classes shown as justified in the accompanying table and requires use to conform to the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995); for example, colours are justified in the cheese mass, certain acidity regulators and preservatives are acceptable, and anti-caking agents are justified for surface/rind treatment only as specified.

Requires products and the milk used in manufacture to comply with maximum levels for contaminants and toxins in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with CAC-established maximum residue limits for veterinary drugs and pesticides in milk.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts, and compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

Heading introducing specific labelling provisions that supplement general Codex labelling standards and cover the name of the food, country of origin, milk fat declaration, date marking and labelling of non-retail containers (detailed in subsections).

Permits use of the name 'Samsø' only when the product conforms to this Standard (alternative spellings allowed where customary); if fat content lies outside the reference range but above the absolute minimum the designation must include an appropriate qualifier or fat content declaration, and the designation may be used for cut, sliced, shredded or grated products made from compliant cheese.

Requires declaration of the country of origin (country of manufacture), and where substantial transformation occurs in a second country that country is to be declared as the origin for labelling purposes.

Requires declaration of milk fat content in a manner acceptable in the country of retail sale, either as percentage by mass, percentage of fat in dry matter, or grams per serving provided the number of servings is stated.
Permits the date of manufacture to be declared instead of minimum durability information where the product is not intended to be purchased as such by the final consumer.
States that labelling of non-retail containers should comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

For checking compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling set out in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the standard's provisions shall be used.
